text: The lower half of a vessel of depth 2d cm is filled with a liquid of refractive index μ₁ and the upper half with a liquid of refractive index μ₂. The apparent depth of the vessel seen perpendicularly is
explanation:
If immiscible liquids of refractive indices \( \mu_1 \) and \( \mu_2 \) are filled in a vessel and their real depths are \( d_1 \) and \( d_2 \) as shown, then apparent depth is given as

\( d_{\text{app}} = \frac{d_1}{\mu_1} + \frac{d_2}{\mu_2} \).

Here, \( d_1 = d_2 = d \).

\( \therefore d_{\text{app}} = \frac{d}{\mu_1} + \frac{d}{\mu_2} = d \left( \frac{1}{\mu_1} + \frac{1}{\mu_2} \right) \).
name: Questions Item 1

text: A ray of light travels from a medium of refractive index 2 to another medium of refractive index \( \sqrt{2} \). Total internal reflection takes place when the angle of incidence is
explanation:
Refractive index of first medium \( \mu_1 = 2 \), second \( \mu_2 = \sqrt{2} \).

For TIR,

\( \frac{\mu_1}{\mu_2} = \frac{1}{\sin i_c} \Rightarrow \sin i_c = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \Rightarrow i_c = 45^\circ \).
name: Questions Item 2

text: A light wave has a frequency of \( 4 \times 10^{14} \) Hz and a wavelength of \( 5 \times 10^{-7} \) m in a medium. The refractive index of the medium is
explanation:
Wavelength in air \( \lambda_{\text{air}} = \frac{c}{\nu} = \frac{3 \times 10^8}{4 \times 10^{14}} = 7.5 \times 10^{-7} \) m.

Refractive index \( \mu = \frac{\lambda_{\text{air}}}{\lambda_{\text{medium}}} = \frac{7.5 \times 10^{-7}}{5 \times 10^{-7}} = 1.5 \).
name: Questions Item 3

text: A prism (\( \mu = 1.5 \)) has the refracting angle of 30°. The deviation of a monochromatic ray incident normally on its one surface will be (given, \( \sin 48^\circ 36' = 0.75 \))
explanation:
For surface AB,

\( \sin e = \mu \sin 30^\circ = 0.75 \Rightarrow e = 48^\circ 36' \).

Deviation \( \delta = e - i = 48^\circ 36' - 30^\circ = 18^\circ 36' \).
name: Questions Item 4

text: A travelling microscope is focused on an ink dot marked on a piece of paper. When a glass slab (\( \mu = 1.5 \)) of thickness 0.12 m is placed on the ink dot. The travelling microscope has to be moved ...... in order to restore the focus
explanation:
Apparent depth \( = \frac{0.12}{1.5} = 0.08 \) m.

Image appears raised by 0.04 m.

Travelling microscope must be moved upward by 0.04 m.
name: Questions Item 5

text: A ray of light is incident normally on a plane mirror. The angle of reflection will be
explanation:
Angle of incidence \( i = 0^\circ \).

Angle of reflection \( r = i = 0^\circ \).
name: Questions Item 6

text: The focal length of a spherical mirror made of steel is 150 cm. If the temperature of the mirror increases by 200 K, its focal length become (coefficient of linear expansion of steel \( \alpha = 12 \times 10^{-6} ^\circ \text{C}^{-1} \))
name: Questions Item 7

text: An object is placed 0.1 m in front of a convex lens of focal length 20 cm made of a material of refractive index 1.5. The surface of the lens away from the object is silvered. If the radius of curvature of the silvered surface is 22 cm, then the distance of the final image from the silvered surface is
explanation:
Power of equivalent mirror \( P = \frac{1}{11} + \frac{1}{20} + \frac{1}{20} \).

Final image distance from silvered surface is 11 cm.
name: Questions Item 8

text: An object is fixed at the bottom of a vessel and water is filled in the vessel up to a height of 10 cm. A plane mirror is placed at a height of 7 cm from the surface of water in such a way that its reflecting surface faces the water. The distance of the image from the mirror is (Refractive index of water, \( \mu = 1.33 \))
explanation:
Apparent depth \( = \frac{10}{1.33} \approx 7.5 \) cm.

Distance from mirror \( = 7 + 7.5 = 14.5 \) cm.
name: Questions Item 9

text: A girl of height 150 cm with her eye level at 140 cm stands in front of plane mirror of height 75 cm fixed to a wall. The lower edge of the mirror is at a height of 85 cm above her feet level. The height of her image the girl can see in the mirror is
name: Questions Item 10

text: A small object is enclosed in a transparent solid sphere of radius 8 cm. The object is situated at 2 cm from the centre of the sphere. If its image appears to be at 3.2 cm from the nearest side, then the refractive index of the material of the sphere is
explanation:
Using refraction formula at spherical surface,

\( \frac{1}{-3.2} - \frac{1}{\mu (-6)} = \frac{1 - \mu}{8} \Rightarrow \mu = 1.50 \).
name: Questions Item 11

text: A thin converging lens of focal length 25 cm forms a sharp image of an object on a screen placed at a distance of 75 cm from the lens. Later the screen is moved closer to the lens by a distance 25 cm. The distance through which the object is to be shifted so that its image on the screen is sharp again is
explanation:
Initial \( u = -37.5 \) cm.

New position at 2f requires object shift 12.5 cm away.
name: Questions Item 12

text: In a spherical glass marble of radius 6 cm, a small air bubble is formed at 1 cm from the centre of the marble. The apparent position of the air bubble from the nearest point on the surface of the marble is about, (refractive index of glass is 1.5)
name: Questions Item 13

text: When an object is moved along the principle axis of a concave mirror placed in air, the image coincides with the object if the object is 50 cm from the mirror. If the mirror is placed at a depth of 20 cm in a transparent medium, the image coincides with the object when the object is 40 cm from the mirror. The refractive index of the liquid is
explanation:
Actual distance outside \( = 30 \) cm, apparent \( = 20 \) cm.

\( \mu = \frac{30}{20} = \frac{3}{2} \).
name: Questions Item 14

text: A light ray incidents normally on one surface of an equilateral prism. The angle of deviation of the light ray is (refractive index of the material of the prism = \( \sqrt{2} \))
name: Questions Item 15

text: As shown in the figure, a parallel beam of light incidents on the upper part of a prism of angle 1.8° and material of refractive index 1.5. The light emerging out from the prism falls on a concave mirror of radius of curvature 40 cm. This distance of the point from the principal axis of the mirror where the light rays are focussed after reflection from the mirror is
name: Questions Item 16
